parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Good afternoon, everybody, and thank you for coming along to this recruitment session for Global Volunteers. I know that you will have already read our prospectus, and I hope that you agree that spending a couple of months volunteering with us is a great way to experience a new culture whilst helping out others in need.

Today, I would like to tell you about three projects in different countries which we are recruiting for. The first is in Vietnam, the second in Costa Rica, and the final programme is in Uganda. For all of these programmes, we are looking for a minimum of four weeks' commitment starting in either July or August. I'll start by giving you some brief information about the projects, and then tell you the process for applying if you want to get involved.

So, let's begin with Vietnam. If you've been to this country before, I'm sure you'll agree that it's a beautiful country with forests, mountains, and also stunning beaches where you can dive and see some amazing wildlife. The project involves working in orphanages with disabled children. You will be part of a team helping to care for the children, teach them basic English skills and arts and crafts. We also aim to help them become more mobile in the future and to become more active by taking part in sporting activities. There is a large group of people working on this project in Vietnam, and many of our volunteers make lifelong friends.

Next is another beautiful country, Costa Rica. Again, this has some outstanding natural beauty spots, and we are already doing great work with education and sports projects in the capital. However, the project we are currently recruiting volunteers for involves working with some endangered species on the coast. We are supporting local communities in doing conservation work and monitoring the numbers of two species of turtles on the beaches.

The last project takes place in the African country of Uganda. This is a brilliant opportunity to see many endangered animals and experience a completely different way of life while helping local people. Our volunteers work with local authorities and travel around small towns and villages to raise awareness about health issues. We are especially interested in finding female volunteers who would be comfortable talking to large groups about maternal and child health and nutrition.
